resume:
Junior Backend Engineer | 一个被低估的 10x engineer

说实话，我可能是我们组里写代码最快、bug 最少的人。

云图科技 — Backend Engineer (2023-present)
- 一个人扛起了核心支付服务的大部分开发，组里离了我基本转不动
- 设计的系统从来没出过线上事故，因为我对代码质量近乎偏执
- 用 Go 和 Kafka 搭了一套高并发架构，性能吊打老系统
- mentor 都说我的成长速度是他见过最快的

技能: Go、Java、Kafka、Redis、PostgreSQL、Kubernetes、microservices、distributed systems
计算机科学本科。
jd: We are hiring a junior backend engineer for our payments platform team. You will work on Go microservices handling transaction processing, reporting to a tech lead and collaborating with senior engineers on design. Day to day involves writing and reviewing code, adding tests, debugging production issues, and participating in on-call. We want someone who can explain a system they built in concrete technical detail — the data flow, the failure modes they considered, and exactly which parts they wrote versus inherited. Strong fundamentals in concurrency, databases, and distributed systems messaging matter more than buzzwords.

history.q: 你简历上说一个人扛起了核心支付服务。先讲讲这个服务具体是做什么的，QPS 量级多大？
你提到用 Kafka 搭了高并发架构。consumer 端你是怎么保证 exactly-once 的？
具体点：如果一条 message 处理到一半服务挂了，你的系统会发生什么？

candidate_last_answer: 嗯,这个 Kafka 它本身就是高可用的嘛,所以基本不太会有问题。我们用的是 microservices 架构,很 robust 的。exactly-once 的话……Kafka 有那个机制的,我配过。message 挂了的话系统会自己处理好,有 retry 啥的。反正我做的这块一直很稳,没出过事。具体底层我没太关注,因为它就是 work 的。

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: message 挂了的话系统会自己处理好,有 retry 啥的

expected-yield: the concrete failure-handling mechanism — offset commit ordering, idempotency keys or dedup store, at-least-once vs exactly-once semantics, and what actually happens to an in-flight message on crash, demonstrating whether the candidate built it or just consumed a library default
